recipes.title: Poisson Cru
recipes.description: Tahitian raw tuna salad cured in lime juice and tossed with coconut milk, cucumber and tomato.
steps: Cut the tuna into clean 1.5 cm cubes and place in a glass bowl.
Pour over the lime juice, season with salt and gently toss to coat every piece.
Refrigerate for 10 minutes only, just to lightly cure the surface of the fish.
Drain off most of the lime juice and discard.
Dice the cucumber and tomato, finely slice the red onion and grate the carrot.
Combine vegetables with the fish and pour over the coconut milk.
Season with pepper and a little more salt, fold gently and serve at once.

recipes.title: Palusami
recipes.description: Samoan parcels of taro leaves wrapped around coconut cream and onion, baked or steamed until silky.
steps: Wash the taro leaves and remove the thick central stems.
Stack five or six leaves in your palm, dipping each one slightly so they cup.
Mix coconut cream with finely chopped onion and salt.
Spoon a generous amount of the coconut cream mixture into the centre of each leaf stack.
Gather the edges of the leaves up to form a parcel that traps the cream inside.
Wrap each parcel in a square of banana leaf and tie with twine.
Bake at 180 C or steam for one hour until the leaves are dark green and meltingly tender.

recipes.title: Sapasui
recipes.description: Samoan version of chop suey: glass noodles braised with soy, ginger and minced beef or chicken.
steps: Soak the glass noodles in warm water for 15 minutes, then drain and snip into shorter lengths.
Heat oil in a large pan and saute diced onion, garlic and grated ginger until fragrant.
Add the minced beef and brown well, breaking up clumps with a spoon.
Pour in soy sauce and beef stock and bring to a simmer.
Add the drained noodles and stir to coat in the sauce.
Simmer uncovered for 10 minutes until the noodles are tender and most of the liquid is absorbed.
Stir through sliced spring onion and serve hot.

recipes.title: Oka I'a
recipes.description: Samoan ceviche of raw fish cubes marinated in lemon juice and dressed with coconut cream and chili.
steps: Cut the fish into 1.5 cm dice and place in a glass bowl.
Pour over lemon juice and salt and chill for 15 minutes until the surface turns opaque.
Drain off about half the lemon juice.
Dice the tomato, cucumber, onion and finely chop the chili.
Add the vegetables to the fish and stir gently.
Pour over the coconut cream and fold to coat everything in the dressing.
Taste, adjust salt and chili to your liking and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Faraoa Coco
recipes.description: Tahitian coconut bread baked in a low oven, faintly sweet with a tender white crumb.
steps: Warm the coconut milk gently with the butter until just melted.
Whisk yeast and sugar into the warm coconut milk and stand for 10 minutes until foamy.
Combine flour, salt and desiccated coconut in a large bowl.
Pour in the wet ingredients and mix to a soft dough, kneading for 8 minutes.
Cover and rise in a warm spot for 90 minutes until doubled.
Knock back, shape into a small loaf and place in a lined tin.
Prove for 30 minutes, then bake at 180 C for 35 minutes until golden and hollow when tapped.

recipes.title: Lu'au Stew
recipes.description: Polynesian stew of tender chicken simmered with cooked taro leaves and creamy coconut milk.
steps: Wash the taro leaves carefully, remove tough stems and chop coarsely.
Boil the leaves in plenty of water for 45 minutes, then drain and rinse.
Heat oil in a heavy pot and brown the chicken thighs on both sides.
Remove the chicken and saute the diced onion and garlic until soft.
Return the chicken with chicken stock and simmer covered for 30 minutes.
Stir in the cooked taro leaves and coconut milk and simmer 20 minutes more.
Season with salt and serve over steamed rice.

recipes.title: Panipopo
recipes.description: Samoan sweet rolls baked sitting in a sea of sweetened coconut sauce that soaks the bottoms.
steps: Whisk yeast with caster sugar and warm milk and stand for 10 minutes until foamy.
Combine flour and salt in a bowl, add the yeast mixture, egg and melted butter.
Knead for 10 minutes to a soft elastic dough, cover and rise for 90 minutes.
Knock back and divide into 12 even balls, arranging snugly in a greased baking dish.
Cover and prove for 30 minutes until pillowy.
Whisk coconut milk with brown sugar and pour evenly over and around the rolls.
Bake at 180 C for 30 minutes until golden on top and the sauce has thickened around the bases.

recipes.title: 'Ota 'Ika
recipes.description: Tongan raw fish salad cured in lemon juice and finished with coconut cream and crisp vegetables.
steps: Dice the fish into 1.5 cm cubes and combine with lemon juice and salt.
Refrigerate for 20 minutes until the fish turns opaque on the outside.
Drain off about half of the lemon juice and discard.
Dice the tomato and cucumber and slice spring onion finely.
Add the vegetables and finely chopped chili to the fish.
Pour over the coconut cream and fold gently to combine.
Chill for 10 minutes more and serve cold as a starter or side.

recipes.title: Fa'apapa
recipes.description: Dense Samoan coconut bread baked into a flat round, sweetened lightly and served torn warm.
steps: Preheat oven to 180 C and grease a round baking tray with butter.
Combine flour, baking powder, sugar, salt and desiccated coconut in a bowl.
Pour in the coconut cream and stir to a stiff but workable dough.
Turn out onto a floured surface and knead briefly until smooth.
Press the dough into a flat disc about 3 cm thick on the prepared tray.
Score the top into wedges and brush with melted butter.
Bake for 35 minutes until golden brown on top and cooked through.
